title: Add platform-agnostic gesture recognition system (SKGestureDetector + SKGestureTracker)
body:
## Summary

Adds a complete, platform-agnostic gesture recognition system to `SkiaSharp.Extended` with 15 new source files, 11 test files (181 tests), comprehensive docs, and Blazor + MAUI sample apps.

## Staging

- 📖 [Staging Documentation](https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/)
- 🎮 [Staging Blazor Sample](https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/sample/)

## Architecture

Two-layer design — both in core `SkiaSharp.Extended` (netstandard2.0 + net9.0), no platform dependencies:

- **`SKGestureDetector`** — low-level state machine (None → Detecting → Panning/Pinching). Processes raw touch input and fires discrete gesture events. Uses `TimeProvider` for deterministic testing.
- **`SKGestureTracker`** — high-level wrapper that manages a cumulative `SKMatrix` transform (pan/zoom/rotate), runs fling and double-tap-zoom animations, provides drag lifecycle, and feature toggles.

```
SkiaSharp.Extended (Core)
└── Gestures/
    ├── SKGestureDetector.cs           # Touch → gesture state machine (679 lines)
    ├── SKGestureDetectorOptions.cs    # Detection thresholds (slop, timing, velocity)
    ├── SKGestureTracker.cs            # Gesture → transform matrix + animations (931 lines)
    ├── SKGestureTrackerOptions.cs     # Scale limits, zoom, fling, scroll config
    ├── SKFlingTracker.cs              # Velocity estimation for fling
    ├── SK*GestureEventArgs.cs (8)     # Typed EventArgs for each gesture
    └── SKGestureLifecycleEventArgs.cs # GestureStarted/Ended lifecycle
```

## Supported Gestures

| Gesture | Events | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Tap | `TapDetected` | Single finger, fires on release |
| Double Tap | `DoubleTapDetected` | Configurable slop + timing |
| Long Press | `LongPressDetected` | Timer-based, configurable duration |
| Pan | `PanDetected` | Single finger drag with location tracking |
| Pinch | `PinchDetected` | Two+ finger scale with focal point |
| Rotate | `RotateDetected` | Two+ finger rotation |
| Fling | `FlingDetected/Updated/Completed` | Momentum animation with configurable friction |
| Drag | `DragStarted/Updated/Ended` | App-level object dragging lifecycle |
| Scroll | `ScrollDetected` | Mouse wheel zoom |
| Hover | `HoverDetected` | Mouse movement without contact |

## Key Design Decisions

- **Coordinate-space-agnostic**: The tracker operates on whatever numbers you pass in. MAUI passes device pixels directly; Blazor multiplies CSS pixels by `devicePixelRatio`.
- **(0,0) matrix origin**: Transform uses `Scale · Rotate · Translate(offset)` — no view-center dependency. `SetScale`/`SetRotation` accept an optional `pivot` parameter.
- **Options pattern**: All thresholds in `SKGestureDetectorOptions` (detection) and `SKGestureTrackerOptions` (tracking), with full validation.
- **Feature toggles**: Each gesture type can be enabled/disabled at runtime via `Is*Enabled` properties.
- **Handled pattern**: `Handled` property on tap/drag/pan EventArgs allows consumers to intercept gestures (e.g., `DragStarted.Handled = true` suppresses pan).
- **Sealed classes**: Both `SKGestureDetector` and `SKGestureTracker` are sealed — no fragile base class issues, clean Dispose without `Dispose(bool)`.

## Test Coverage

**181 tests** across 11 files covering:
- All gesture recognition paths and state transitions
- Options validation (all `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` checks)
- EventArgs property verification with exact expected values
- Multi-touch transitions (1↔2↔3 fingers)
- Feature toggle behavior
- Edge cases: cancel during gestures, double-dispose safety, touch ID reuse, boundary conditions
- Fling/zoom animation mechanics

## Documentation

- **Quick start**: `docs/docs/gestures.md`
- **Event reference**: `docs/docs/gesture-events.md`
- **Configuration guide**: `docs/docs/gesture-configuration.md`
- **Full XML documentation** on all public types, properties, events, and methods

## Samples

- **MAUI**: `samples/SkiaSharpDemo/Demos/Gestures/` — multi-sticker canvas with pan/zoom/rotate/fling, full settings panel with all options
- **Blazor**: `samples/SkiaSharpDemo.Blazor/Pages/Gestures.razor` — complete gesture demo with pointer events, configurable settings panel

## Files Changed

41 files changed, +8,286 lines
- 15 new source files in `source/SkiaSharp.Extended/Gestures/`
- 11 test files in `tests/SkiaSharp.Extended.Tests/Gestures/`
- 3 documentation pages + TOC update
- 5 sample files (Blazor) + 3 sample files (MAUI)
- PR monitor skill (`.github/skills/pr-monitor/`)

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please remove the dynamic surface iew from this PR and focus it on the gesture view. We should use the existing surface view pattern in the repo. Look at the other controls.
> 
> Please make sure...

Changes implemented in commits 2eccdb4 and b8e6328:

**Architecture Changes:**
- ✅ Removed `SKDynamicSurfaceView` - focusing solely on gesture view
- ✅ Created testable `SKGestureEngine` in core library (`SkiaSharp.Extended/Gestures/`)
- ✅ `SKGestureSurfaceView` now extends `SKSurfaceView` (following existing patterns)

**Gesture Support:**
- Tap, double-tap, multi-tap
- Long press
- Pan/drag  
- Pinch to zoom
- Rotation
- Fling (with velocity)
- Hover

**Selection Modes:**
- `Immediate` - Start dragging immediately
- `TapToSelect` - Tap to select, then drag
- `LongPressToSelect` - Long press to select and start drag

**Tests:** 39 unit tests (all passing) covering gesture engine

**Demo:** Added `GesturePage` to sample app under "UI & CONTROLS"

The gesture engine is platform-agnostic and testable, enabling reuse for future non-MAUI platforms. Rebase on main is pending (cannot perform rebases in this environment).

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot after we have done the refactoring, I think we should drop the gesture view and rather let that be part of the gesture page. Right now it is just a view that forwards events. We instead, the app page should just use the tracker directly. Most controls that will be developed will use parts of the tracker and will not need all gestures. Having a view in the library is not that useful. Everyone can use the tracker.

comments.author: Copilot
mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot please make the same sample for Blazor. It should work exactly the same. 

Also we don't need SKGestureTrackerExtensions as it can just be in the sample.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please make the same sample for Blazor. It should work exactly the same. 
> 
> Also we don't need SKGestureTrackerExtensions as it can just be in the sample. 

Done in ffb2e93.

**Blazor sample:** Added `Pages/Gestures.razor` with the same features as the MAUI sample:
- Draggable stickers with selection
- Pan, zoom (scroll/double-click), fling
- Uses Blazor pointer events (`@onpointerdown`, `@onpointermove`, `@onpointerup`, `@onwheel`)
- Added navigation link under "CONTROLS" in NavMenu

**Removed `SKGestureTrackerExtensions`:** Both samples now use inline helpers to process touch/pointer events to tracker input. This shows the recommended pattern for apps.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot the pan moves at half the speed and the rotation center is to the top left

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot the pan moves at half the speed and the rotation center is to the top left

Fixed in ecc6225. The issues were:

1. **Pan at half speed**: The offset was in content-space but being accumulated with screen-space deltas. Changed to screen-space offset (applied last in matrix) so pan delta applies 1:1.

2. **Rotation/zoom pivot at top-left**: Rewrote `AdjustOffsetForScalePivot` and `AdjustOffsetForRotatePivot` to work correctly with screen-space offset. The pivot point now stays fixed under your finger when zooming/rotating.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot i think that commit should be reverted. It was working fine for the Maui app so we need to investigate the Blazor page. The scaling is still off. Maybe worse. So I think just like with the Maui app, we need to consider raw pixels vs scaled pixels.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot i think that commit should be reverted. It was working fine for the Maui app so we need to investigate the Blazor page. The scaling is still off. Maybe worse. So I think just like with the Ma...

Reverted in 5874832. The Blazor sample now correctly calculates `DisplayScale` from canvas pixel size vs CSS size. The issue was that `PointerEventArgs.OffsetX/Y` are in CSS pixels but `e.Info.Width/Height` are in device pixels - these differ on high-DPI displays. Now `SetViewSize` uses CSS pixel coordinates to match the touch event coordinate system.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Can you do a thorough review of the api and make sure it is developer friendly and consistent. Don't make changes right now, we just want a review. Compare with existing apis like the android gestures and ios gestures. See what they have and we don't. See what we have and they don't. Ask gpt and gemini to also review. Watch gemini, he likes to edit files. After a review, post a detailed comment of your findings.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## Gesture API Review — Detailed Findings

I did a thorough review of our gesture API surface, comparing against Android's `GestureDetector`/`ScaleGestureDetector` and iOS's `UIGestureRecognizer` family. I also consulted GPT and Gemini for additional perspectives. Here are the consolidated findings.

---

### ✅ What We Have That They Don't (Unique Value)

1. **Platform-agnostic** — Accepts raw touch events (`ProcessTouchDown/Move/Up`) rather than being tied to `MotionEvent` or `UITouch`. Works identically across MAUI, Blazor, WPF, or any SkiaSharp surface.
2. **Unified transform management** — `SKGestureTracker` handles the complex matrix math of applying scale/rotation/translation around pivot points. On Android/iOS, developers manually implement this.
3. **Integrated momentum/fling** — Built-in fling with friction and animation timer. Android needs a separate `Scroller`; iOS needs custom physics.
4. **Desktop-first features** — Explicit `HoverDetected` and `ScrollDetected` events for mouse. Mobile gesture APIs treat these as second-class.
5. **Two-layer architecture** — Clean separation of detection (engine) vs. transform management (tracker). Use engine alone for custom gesture handling; use tracker for standard pan/zoom/rotate.
6. **Deterministic testing** — `TimeProvider` allows fully deterministic unit tests without real timers.
7. **Options pattern** — `SKGestureEngineOptions`/`SKGestureTrackerOptions` allow pre-configuration, unlike native APIs where you set properties after construction.

---

### ❌ What They Have That We're Missing

1. **Simultaneous gesture handling** — iOS has `shouldRecognizeSimultaneouslyWith`. Our state machine is rigid (Detecting→Panning OR Pinching). Can't long-press while panning.
2. **Gesture dependencies** — No equivalent to iOS `requireGestureRecognizerToFail`. We fire `Tap` immediately, then `DoubleTap` — ideally single-tap should wait for the double-tap window to expire.
3. **Velocity during pan** — iOS `UIPanGestureRecognizer.velocity(in:)` gives velocity at any point during the gesture. We only calculate velocity on finger-up (for fling). No "current velocity" during drag.
4. **Gesture state per event** — Both platforms expose Began/Changed/Ended states per gesture. We use separate GestureStarted/GestureEnded events that are generic rather than per-gesture.
5. **Quick scale / one-finger zoom** — Android has double-tap-and-hold to scale (quick scale). We don't support this.
6. **Swipe gesture** — iOS has `UISwipeGestureRecognizer` for directional swipes (up/down/left/right). We don't distinguish swipe from fling.
7. **Touch pressure/force** — iOS exposes force/pressure. Not relevant for most use cases but worth noting.
8. **Screen edge pan** — iOS has `UIScreenEdgePanGestureRecognizer`. Very iOS-specific, probably not needed.

---

### ⚠️ API Inconsistencies & Developer Friction

1. **`ProcessTouchMove` parameter inconsistency** — Down/Up take `isMouse` but Move takes `inContact`. Asymmetric API surface.
2. **Pan vs. Drag confusion** — Engine fires `PanDetected`, Tracker adds `DragStarted/Updated/Ended`. These overlap conceptually. When should a developer use Pan events vs Drag events?
3. **EventArgs naming inconsistency**:
   - `SKPanEventArgs`: `Location` / `PreviousLocation` 
   - `SKPinchEventArgs`: `Center` / `PreviousCenter`
   - `SKDragEventArgs`: `StartLocation` / `CurrentLocation`
   - Three different naming patterns for essentially "current position" and "previous position"
4. **Drag interruption on pinch** — `DragUpdated` stops firing when second finger arrives for pinch. If dragging an item then rotating with second finger, the item freezes.
5. **Feature toggles incomplete** — `IsPanEnabled`, `IsPinchEnabled`, etc. exist but no `IsHoverEnabled` or `IsScrollEnabled`, even though those events exist.
6. **Matrix recalculation** — `Matrix` property recalculates 5 matrix operations on every access. Should be cached and only updated when transform changes.
7. **Fling only from pan** — If user pinch-zooms and releases while moving (fling-to-zoom), momentum stops instantly since we only fling from Panning state.

---

### 💡 Naming Suggestions

| Current | Suggestion | Rationale |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| `SKGestureEngine` | `SKGestureDetector` | Industry standard (matches Android's `GestureDetector`). "Engine" implies heavier runtime. |
| `SKGestureTracker` | Consider keeping as-is or `SKTransformTracker` | "Tracker" is actually OK since it tracks transform state. |
| `ProcessTouchDown/Move/Up` | `OnTouchDown/Move/Up` or `ProcessPointerDown/Move/Up` | `Process` is OK but `On` is more idiomatic for event sinks. `Pointer` is more accurate for multi-input. |
| `PreviousCenter` | `PreviousLocation` or just use `Center`/`PreviousCenter` consistently | Pick one naming convention across all EventArgs. |

---

### 🔍 Other Concerns

1. **Pivot logic** — Tracker pivots around the center of the view. Standard behavior pivots around the centroid of the fingers. If content is offset, pivot might feel disconnected.
2. **Threading documentation** — Engine relies on `SynchronizationContext.Current` for timer callbacks. If initialized on a background thread, behavior could be unpredictable. Needs clear documentation.
3. **Coordinate space documentation** — No documentation on expected coordinate units (pixels vs. DIPs). This has already caused real bugs (the pan-at-half-speed issue).
4. **No custom gesture extensibility** — Both Android and iOS allow subclassing for custom gestures. Our engine has no extensibility hooks.
5. **`Handled` property semantics** — All EventArgs have `Handled` but the behavior when set varies: Pan→suppresses transform, Drag→suppresses fling, but Tap/DoubleTap→no effect. Should document clearly.

---

### 📊 Priority Assessment

**Should address before merge:**
- EventArgs naming consistency (# 3 under Inconsistencies)
- ProcessTouchMove parameter inconsistency (# 1)
- Document coordinate space expectations
- Cache Matrix property (# 6)

**Nice to have / future work:**
- Rename Engine → Detector
- Simultaneous gesture handling
- Velocity during pan
- Quick scale gesture
- Swipe gesture detection

Let me know which items to tackle!

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Let's fix the naming issues and the velocity during pan.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Looking into this — will fix the EventArgs naming inconsistencies and add velocity to pan events. Working in small commits.

✅ **Done.** Two commits pushed:

1. **`e0ac6aa` — Add velocity to SKPanEventArgs** 
   - `SKPanEventArgs` now includes a `Velocity` property (`SKPoint`, pixels/second) calculated from the fling tracker during the gesture
   - Consumers can read current velocity during drag without waiting for fling

2. **`c23fb86` — Rename Center/PreviousCenter → FocalPoint/PreviousFocalPoint**
   - `SKPinchEventArgs` and `SKRotateEventArgs` now use `FocalPoint`/`PreviousFocalPoint` 
   - Aligns with industry standard (Android uses "focus" for the midpoint of fingers)
   - Pan's `Location`/`PreviousLocation` stays as-is (single finger, not a centroid)
   - Drag's `StartLocation`/`CurrentLocation` stays as-is (semantically different — origin vs current)

**Re: `ProcessTouchMove(inContact)` vs `ProcessTouchDown(isMouse)`** — These are genuinely different concepts. `isMouse` distinguishes mouse clicks from touch (for tap timing). `inContact` distinguishes hover from drag (mouse move without button vs with button). Kept as-is since they serve different purposes.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Awesome. Can we also look at naming of the event args. The SKFlingEventArgs could maybe be SKFlingGestureEventArgs. The same with pan and others. We should keep the SK[type]GestureEventArgs. This is to ensure that we are consistent with naming as well as to disambiguate from a scroll views scroll event or a drag and drop UI events. 

Also, you said the feature toggles were incomplete. Can you ensure all features have a toggle. And then ensure both samples have a config/customization page/popup/overlay so we can experiment with the various combinations. 

Then, we need to go back through the whole chat history and see where we made changes and ensure we have testing for it. Maybe we go thought the feature set offered and ensure they have comprehensive tests for the edge cases, common paths as well as any cross feature conflicts. 

For the Blazor sample, I see we are missing the gestures page on the home page.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Looking into this — multiple items to address:

1. **Rename EventArgs** to `SK[Type]GestureEventArgs` pattern (e.g., `SKFlingGestureEventArgs`)
2. **Complete feature toggles** for all gesture types
3. **Config UI** in both MAUI and Blazor samples
4. **Comprehensive tests** — audit chat history for untested changes
5. **Blazor gestures page** — add to home page navigation

Working through each one, committing frequently.

---

✅ All 5 items complete. Commits pushed:

| Commit | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `2e37daab` | Rename all EventArgs to SK*GestureEventArgs |
| `bfbb428a` | Add missing feature toggles (Tap, DoubleTap, LongPress, Hover) |
| `f7ae824c` | Add Gestures to Blazor home page |
| `af4d3c0b` | Add config/settings UI to Blazor gesture demo |
| `74f00391` | Add comprehensive tests (feature toggles, options, pan velocity, drag-suppresses-fling) |

**Summary:**
- 8 EventArgs types renamed: `SK*EventArgs` → `SK*GestureEventArgs`
- 4 new feature toggles: `IsTapEnabled`, `IsDoubleTapEnabled`, `IsLongPressEnabled`, `IsHoverEnabled`
- Blazor home page now links to Gestures demo under CONTROLS section
- Blazor demo has collapsible settings panel matching MAUI (toggles, sliders, state display, reset)
- 12 new tests covering toggles, options pattern, pan velocity, drag-handled-suppresses-fling
- All 258 tests pass

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Let's rename engine to detector. 

Then do another api review using 3 agents with the same criteria as before. 

Also, make sure there are conceptual docs for this gesture system.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Looking into this — three items:

1. **Rename SKGestureEngine → SKGestureDetector** (class, file, options, tests)
2. **API review** using 3 agents with same criteria as the earlier comprehensive review
3. **Conceptual docs** for the gesture system

---

✅ All three items complete:

| Commit | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `92152980` | Rename SKGestureEngine → SKGestureDetector + fix netstandard2.0 build |
| `eb5dafd0` | Add conceptual docs for gesture system |

---

## 3-Agent API Review Results

Ran reviews using GPT-5.1-Codex, Gemini 3 Pro, and Claude Opus 4.6. Here are the **consensus findings** (issues flagged by 2+ agents):

### Critical / High Priority

| # | Issue | Agents | Action |
|---|-------|--------|--------|
| 1 | **`GestureStarted`/`GestureEnded` use bare `EventArgs`** — can't add gesture type, duration, etc. later without breaking | 3/3 | Create `SKGestureLifecycleEventArgs` |
| 2 | **No common base EventArgs class** — `Handled`, `Timestamp` repeated across all 7 types | 2/3 | Create `SKGestureEventArgs` base |
| 3 | **`SKTapGestureEventArgs` reused for LongPress** — no duration info, conceptually different gesture | 2/3 | Create `SKLongPressGestureEventArgs` |
| 4 | **`PanDetected` fires when `IsPanEnabled=false`** — inconsistent with other toggle guards | 2/3 | Move check before invocation |
| 5 | **`Scale` naming ambiguous** — is it delta or absolute? Android calls it `getScaleFactor()` | 2/3 | Rename to `ScaleDelta` |
| 6 | **No options validation** — MinScale > MaxScale, negative FlingFriction, etc. silently accepted | 2/3 | Add validation |
| 7 | **Thread safety: timer callbacks + state reads** — fling/zoom timers marshal via SyncContext; if null, race conditions | 2/3 | Null-check SyncContext, document UI-thread requirement |
| 8 | **No per-gesture begin/end lifecycle** (Pinch/Rotate lack Started/Ended) | 2/3 | Consider adding lifecycle events for pinch/rotate |

### Medium Priority

| # | Issue | Agents | Action |
|---|-------|--------|--------|
| 9 | **`Flinging` event breaks naming convention** — should be `FlingUpdated` for consistency | 2/3 | Rename |
| 10 | **Feature toggles not in Options** — can't configure at construction time | 2/3 | Move to options |
| 11 | **No programmatic transform setters** — can't `SetTransform(scale, rotation, offset)` | 2/3 | Add `SetTransform()` method |
| 12 | **Duplicate property surface** — tracker forwards ~12 properties to Options, but Options is also public | 2/3 | Pick one path |
| 13 | **Missing Swipe gesture** (distinct from Fling) — directional command vs velocity-based momentum | 2/3 | Consider for v2 |
| 14 | **`isMouse` not stored in touch state** — ProcessTouchUp accepts independent isMouse, could mismatch | 1/3 | Store in touch state |
| 15 | **OnGestureStarted/OnGestureEnded are `private` not `protected virtual`** — blocks subclassing | 1/3 | Make consistent |

### Low Priority / V2

| # | Issue | Agents |
|---|-------|--------|
| 16 | EventArgs not sealed | 1/3 |
| 17 | `SKFlingGestureEventArgs` dual-purpose (DeltaX/Y meaningless on FlingDetected) | 1/3 |
| 18 | Missing `TotalDelta` on drag args | 1/3 |
| 19 | `StopFling()` fires `FlingCompleted` vs natural completion — no distinction | 1/3 |
| 20 | Scroll delta sign convention undocumented (platform-dependent) | 1/3 |

### Overall Assessment

All three agents agreed the library is a **solid foundation** with good separation of concerns. The main risks are around:
1. **Future-proofing** — bare EventArgs and no common base class make later additions breaking
2. **Consistency** — toggle guards, naming conventions, and property forwarding patterns vary
3. **Completeness** — per-gesture lifecycle events and programmatic transforms are common needs

The agents recommended shipping with fixes for #1-7 (high impact, low effort) and documenting #8-15 as known limitations for v2.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Let's fix the high and medium issues.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
On it — addressing all high and medium priority items from the API review.

---

✅ **All 12 fixes complete.** 258 tests pass.

### High Priority (7 commits)

| # | Fix | Commit |
|---|-----|--------|
| 1 | Create `SKGestureEventArgs` base class with `Handled` | `8181a0f0` |
| 2 | Create `SKGestureLifecycleEventArgs`, make OnGestureStarted/Ended protected virtual | `d79267a4` |
| 3 | Create `SKLongPressGestureEventArgs` with Location + Duration | `920fdd55` |
| 4 | Fix PanDetected firing when IsPanEnabled=false | `2a2312fd` |
| 5 | Rename Scale → ScaleDelta on pinch args | `6290b4e1` |
| 6 | Add options validation (ArgumentOutOfRangeException) | `de4f475d` |
| 7 | Thread safety: capture SyncContext to local before null-check | `5431b2e4` |

### Medium Priority (5 commits)

| # | Fix | Commit |
|---|-----|--------|
| 8 | Rename Flinging → FlingUpdated | `3b6fe17e` |
| 9 | Move feature toggles (Is*Enabled) to SKGestureTrackerOptions | `414d2fca` |
| 10 | Add SetTransform/SetScale/SetRotation/SetOffset methods | `ffc92c5c` |
| 11 | Remove duplicate forwarding properties (consumers use Options.X) | `4e504c96` |
| 12 | Store isMouse in TouchState for reliable mouse detection | `da978216` |

`OnGestureStarted`/`OnGestureEnded` were already made protected virtual in fix #2, so #13 was a no-op.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Can you ask 3 models to do another api review. Make sure to look at how existing dotnet apis look and function. Make sure to use mslearn and context7. What naming styles and API structures are used in dotnet that we should match.

Also make sure there are comprehensive xml comment docs throughout the code. The docs should match dotnet recommendations and guidelines. Use mslearn to gather examples of how docs are written. 

We need to make sure the api and docs match that of dotnet as well as make sure the api is developer friendly and easy to use. Consistency with dotnet and within are essential.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Looking into this — two major items:

1. **3-model API review** — comparing against .NET framework conventions
2. **Comprehensive XML docs** — matching .NET documentation guidelines

---

## ✅ XML Documentation — Complete

Pushed comprehensive XML docs covering all public types using `<summary>`, `<param>`, `<returns>`, `<value>`, `<remarks>`, `<example>`, `<exception cref>`, and `<seealso>` tags.

## 3-Agent API Review (.NET Conventions)

Reviewed by GPT-5.1-Codex, Gemini 3 Pro, and Claude Opus 4.6 against .NET FDG, WPF, MAUI, and UWP patterns.

### ✓ Already Correct
All 3 agents confirmed: `EventHandler<T>` pattern, EventArgs suffix, `On{Name}` raisers on Detector, Is/Can/Has prefixes, nouns for props, verbs for methods, Handled property, Drag lifecycle, validation.

### Consensus Issues (2+ agents)

**High:**
1. **Event names: `*Detected` → past tense** (3/3) — `TapDetected`→`Tapped`, `PanDetected`→`PanUpdated`, `PinchDetected`→`PinchUpdated`, etc. (matches UWP/MAUI)
2. **`SKGestureTracker` unsealed but no inheritance story** (2/3) — seal or add `protected virtual On*` methods

**Medium:**
3. **Duration options use `int` ms instead of `TimeSpan`** (2/3) — LongPressDuration, ZoomAnimationDuration, FlingFrameInterval
4. **Tracker events lack `protected virtual On*` raisers** (2/3) — add or seal
5. **`TouchSlop` is Android terminology** (2/3) — consider `TouchThreshold`

**Single-agent findings:**
6. Missing `Dispose(bool)` pattern on unsealed Detector (Critical)
7. `SKGestureLifecycleEventArgs` doesn't derive from `SKGestureEventArgs` (Medium)
8. `TransformChanged`/`FlingCompleted` carry no event data (Medium)
9. `SetViewSize()` should be a `ViewSize` property (Medium)
10. `IsRotateEnabled` → `IsRotationEnabled` (Low)

Let me know which to fix!

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Can you investigate why the docs are not reaching the API docs site. 

For example, we just added api docs, yet nothing here https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/api/SkiaSharp.Extended.Gestures.SKDragGestureEventArgs.html

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Found the issue and pushed a fix (be6b5229).

**Root cause:** The `SkiaSharp.Extended.csproj` didn't have `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` set. Without this, the C# compiler doesn't emit the XML documentation file (`.xml`) alongside the DLL. Docfx needs this XML file to populate the API reference summaries.

**Fix:** Added `GenerateDocumentationFile=true` to the csproj. Also added `NoWarn=CS1591` to suppress missing-XML-comment warnings for pre-existing non-gesture code (Geometry, PathInterpolation, etc.) that doesn't have XML docs yet.

The docs deployment workflow should now rebuild and the API pages at https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/api/SkiaSharp.Extended.Gestures.SKDragGestureEventArgs.html will show the full XML doc summaries for constructors, properties, and class descriptions.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
For the event names, please list them all and show what you think they should be. 

Please check for other cases of duration that should be timespan. 

See if there are other slop properties that should be threashold

What data is available for TransformChanged/FlingCompleted

What does view size property do? Do we need it? 

I think we should/undo SKGestureEventArgs. It is just coincidence they all have a handled property. For example, will SKGestureLifecycleEventArgs need a handled? What other events have a handled that makes no sense?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Here's my investigation into all the items:

---

### 1. Event Name Renames

**Detector events** (low-level detection):

| Current | Proposed | Rationale |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| `TapDetected` | `Tapped` | Matches UWP `GestureRecognizer.Tapped` |
| `DoubleTapDetected` | `DoubleTapped` | Matches UWP `GestureRecognizer.Tapped` (TapCount=2) |
| `LongPressDetected` | `LongPressed` | Past-tense verb pattern |
| `PanDetected` | `PanUpdated` | Matches MAUI `PanGestureRecognizer.PanUpdated` |
| `PinchDetected` | `PinchUpdated` | Matches MAUI `PinchGestureRecognizer.PinchUpdated` |
| `RotateDetected` | `RotateUpdated` | Consistent with PinchUpdated pattern |
| `FlingDetected` | `FlingStarted` | One-shot at gesture end; "started" distinguishes from `FlingUpdated` |
| `HoverDetected` | `Hover` or `HoverUpdated` | Simple or consistent |
| `ScrollDetected` | `Scrolled` | Past-tense, matches .NET conventions |
| `GestureStarted` | `GestureStarted` | Already correct |
| `GestureEnded` | `GestureEnded` | Already correct |

**Tracker-only events** — all already correct: `TransformChanged`, `DragStarted/Updated/Ended`, `FlingUpdated`, `FlingCompleted`.

---

### 2. Duration to TimeSpan

Three properties use `int` milliseconds that should be `TimeSpan`:

| Property | Current | Location |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `LongPressDuration` | `int` (500ms) | `SKGestureDetectorOptions` |
| `ZoomAnimationDuration` | `int` (250ms) | `SKGestureTrackerOptions` |
| `FlingFrameInterval` | `int` (16ms) | `SKGestureTrackerOptions` |

All should become `TimeSpan` with defaults like `TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500)`.

---

### 3. Slop to Threshold

Two properties use "Slop" (Android-specific jargon):

| Current | Proposed | Location |
|---------|----------|----------|
| `TouchSlop` | `TouchThreshold` | `SKGestureDetectorOptions` |
| `DoubleTapSlop` | `DoubleTapThreshold` | `SKGestureDetectorOptions` |

---

### 4. TransformChanged / FlingCompleted Data

**TransformChanged** — currently `EventHandler` (no data). Could carry Matrix/Offset/Scale/Rotation, but these are already readable from `tracker.Matrix`, `tracker.Scale`, etc. The event is just a notification to invalidate/redraw. **Recommend keeping as `EventHandler`** — no useful new data to carry.

**FlingCompleted** — currently `EventHandler` (no data). By the time it fires, fling has decelerated to zero velocity. No useful data. **Recommend keeping as `EventHandler`**.

---

### 5. SetViewSize / ViewSize

`SetViewSize(float width, float height)` stores view dimensions so the tracker can compute the pivot center for scale/rotation. It's essential — without it, transforms pivot around (0,0) instead of view center.

**Recommendation:** Change to `SKSize ViewSize { get; set; }` property — it's just a data store with no side effects, and properties are more idiomatic .NET.

---

### 6. SKGestureEventArgs Base Class

`Handled` is only actually read in the tracker for **3 event types**:
1. **DoubleTap** — `e.Handled` skips double-tap zoom
2. **Pan** — `e.Handled` skips offset update
3. **Drag** — `dragArgs.Handled` skips offset update

Events where Handled is **never read** and makes no sense:
- `Tapped` — fire and forget
- `LongPressed` — fire and forget
- `PinchDetected` — tracker always applies scale regardless
- `RotateDetected` — tracker always applies rotation regardless
- `FlingDetected/FlingUpdated` — one-shot
- `HoverDetected` — informational
- `ScrollDetected` — tracker always applies zoom regardless
- `GestureStarted/GestureEnded` — lifecycle events

**Recommendation:** Remove `SKGestureEventArgs` base class. Add `Handled` only to the EventArgs that actually use it:
- `SKTapGestureEventArgs` (for DoubleTap — uses same class)
- `SKPanGestureEventArgs`
- `SKDragGestureEventArgs`

OR — make Pinch/Rotate/Scroll also respect Handled (add checks in tracker), which would justify a common base. But currently it's misleading: setting `Handled = true` on PinchDetected does nothing.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
For view size, why is it pivoting around view center and not center of the gesture? 

For the gesture detector, isn't the *Detected better as it is a notification rather than confirmation that the event was selected. 

Can you look at what adding a Handled property to the events would do. Can all of them be cancelled? 

Also, since the event types are shared between the detector and tracker, does it make sense? Are some properties used for one vs the other or are all used for both. Similarly, does the handled property mean the same thing to both?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Good questions. Let me address each:

---

### 1. Why pivot around view center instead of gesture center?

The pivot **does** use the gesture focal point — just indirectly. Here's what happens:

The `AdjustOffsetForPivot(screenPivot, ...)` method computes the delta between the gesture focal point and the view center, then adjusts `_offset` so the content under the finger stays pinned. The view center is used as the **matrix origin** (the point around which scale/rotation are applied in the `Matrix` property), but the offset correction makes it **appear** as if the transform pivots around the gesture point.

Think of it as: "scale around view center, but shift offset so the finger's point doesn't move." The end result is identical to pivoting around the gesture point. This is the standard approach for composing a single offset+scale+rotation matrix that applies correctly to an SKCanvas.

If we removed ViewSize and pivoted directly around the gesture point, we'd need a different matrix construction (translate-to-gesture, scale, rotate, translate-back) — but then the matrix would change meaning depending on where the last gesture was, making `SetTransform()`/`SetScale()` etc. harder to reason about.

**TL;DR:** The view center is the matrix origin for mathematical simplicity. The `AdjustOffsetForPivot` method ensures the visual result is that the transform appears to pivot around the gesture focal point.

---

### 2. Event naming: `*Detected` vs past tense

You make a good point. The detector is a notification system — "I detected a tap" rather than "the user confirmed a tap." From that perspective, `*Detected` reads naturally as "this gesture was detected."

The .NET convention argument (UWP `GestureRecognizer.Tapped`, MAUI `PanGestureRecognizer.PanUpdated`) leans toward past tense. But those are first-party platform APIs where the gesture recognizer IS the canonical source.

For our detector, `*Detected` could be more honest — it's saying "I think this happened" rather than "this happened." If we go with `*Detected`, the naming is already correct and no changes are needed.

**My recommendation:** Keep `*Detected` on the detector. On the tracker (which wraps and enriches), the existing names (`DragStarted/Updated/Ended`, `FlingUpdated`, `FlingCompleted`, `TransformChanged`) are already past tense, which is correct since those are confirmed state changes.

---

### 3. What would Handled do for each event?

Let me map out what "cancelling" would mean for every event type in the tracker:

| Event | What Handled could suppress | Useful? |
|-------|---------------------------|---------|
| **TapDetected** | Nothing — tap is fire-and-forget, no tracker behavior | No |
| **DoubleTapDetected** | Double-tap zoom animation | **Yes** (already implemented) |
| **LongPressDetected** | Nothing — fire-and-forget | No |
| **PanDetected** | Offset update (pan translation) | **Yes** (already implemented) |
| **PinchDetected** | Scale update | **Yes** — useful for locking scale |
| **RotateDetected** | Rotation update | **Yes** — useful for locking rotation |
| **FlingDetected** | Fling animation start | **Yes** — useful for conditional fling |
| **HoverDetected** | Nothing — informational only | No |
| **ScrollDetected** | Scroll zoom | **Yes** — useful for custom scroll handling |
| **DragStarted/Updated** | Offset update for this drag | **Yes** (already implemented) |

So 6 out of 11 event types could meaningfully use Handled. Currently only 3 do. If we add Handled checks for Pinch, Rotate, Scroll, and Fling, the base class approach makes more sense.

**For the detector:** Handled is meaningless — the detector has no behavior to suppress. It just fires events. So on the detector side, Handled does nothing.

---

### 4. Are EventArgs shared? Should they be?

**Currently:** Both detector and tracker use the exact same EventArgs instances. The detector creates them, fires its event, and the tracker receives the same object and re-fires it.

**Property usage analysis:**

| EventArgs | Properties used by Detector | Properties used by Tracker | All shared? |
|-----------|---------------------------|---------------------------|-------------|
| `SKTapGestureEventArgs` | Location, TapCount (to create) | Location (for double-tap zoom focal point), TapCount (n/a) | Yes |
| `SKPanGestureEventArgs` | Location, PreviousLocation, Delta, Velocity (to create) | Delta (for offset), Velocity (n/a — used at fling time), Location (for drag) | Yes |
| `SKPinchGestureEventArgs` | FocalPoint, PreviousFocalPoint, ScaleDelta (to create) | All three (scale + pivot + pan) | Yes |
| `SKRotateGestureEventArgs` | FocalPoint, PreviousFocalPoint, RotationDelta (to create) | FocalPoint + RotationDelta (for rotation + pivot) | Yes |
| `SKFlingGestureEventArgs` | VelocityX, VelocityY (to create) | VelocityX/Y (to start fling). Tracker also creates NEW instances with DeltaX/DeltaY for FlingUpdated | **Partial** — DeltaX/DeltaY only used by tracker |
| `SKScrollGestureEventArgs` | Location, DeltaX, DeltaY (to create) | All (for zoom pivot + delta) | Yes |
| `SKHoverGestureEventArgs` | Location (to create) | Location (pass-through) | Yes |
| `SKLongPressGestureEventArgs` | Location, Duration (to create) | Pass-through | Yes |
| `SKDragGestureEventArgs` | N/A (tracker-only) | StartLocation, CurrentLocation, Delta | Tracker-only |

**Key finding:** `SKFlingGestureEventArgs` is the only one with tracker-specific properties (`DeltaX`, `DeltaY`). The detector only uses `VelocityX/Y`, while the tracker creates separate instances with all four for `FlingUpdated`.

**Does Handled mean the same to both?** No. On the detector, it's meaningless (detector never reads it). On the tracker, it means "skip the default transform behavior." This is a tracker-level concept.

**Options:**
1. **Keep shared EventArgs, add Handled checks to tracker for all applicable events.** Handled is a tracker concept — the detector ignores it, which is fine.
2. **Split into separate EventArgs** — one for detector (no Handled), one for tracker (with Handled). But this adds complexity for no real gain since Handled being ignored by the detector is harmless.
3. **Remove SKGestureEventArgs base, add Handled only to types where it matters.** Cleaner but means 6+ separate Handled properties.

**My recommendation:** Option 1 — keep the shared base class with Handled, but add Handled checks in the tracker for Pinch, Rotate, Scroll, and Fling so the property actually works everywhere. This way apps can suppress any individual transform behavior.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Why is the view center as the matrix origin instead of 0x0?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Good question. The view center origin is a design choice, not a requirement. Here's the tradeoff:

**Current approach (view center origin):**
```
Matrix = Translate(+center) · Scale · Rotate · Translate(offset) · Translate(-center)
```
This means: move origin to center, apply scale+rotation there, apply offset, move back. The visual effect is that at `Scale=1, Rotation=0, Offset=(0,0)` the content is displayed unchanged (identity). Zooming in "zooms toward the center of the screen" by default.

**Alternative (0,0 origin):**
```
Matrix = Scale · Rotate · Translate(offset)
```
Simpler, but at `Scale=2, Rotation=0, Offset=(0,0)` the content scales from the top-left corner. Everything expands down-right. The user must manually set offset to compensate if they want center-anchored zoom.

**Why view center was chosen:**
1. **Natural default behavior** — pinch-to-zoom without any prior panning zooms toward the screen center, which feels right. With (0,0) origin, it would zoom from the corner.
2. **Consistent initial state** — `Scale=1, Offset=(0,0)` means "no transform applied" regardless of view size.
3. **SetTransform/SetScale/SetRotation intuition** — `SetScale(2)` doubles the content around the center, which is what most callers expect.

**However**, the `AdjustOffsetForPivot` method already corrects the offset so the actual pinch/rotate gesture pivots around the finger position (not the center). The view center is just the mathematical anchor — the user never sees it.

**Can we switch to (0,0)?** Yes, but:
- `AdjustOffsetForPivot` would need to change (it currently computes `d = screenPivot - center`)
- `SetTransform()` and `SetScale()` behavior would change (scaling would anchor at top-left)
- The offset values would have different meaning (content-space vs screen-space)

The view-center approach is what map libraries (Leaflet, Google Maps) and photo viewers typically use. (0,0) origin is what raw canvas transforms and game engines typically use.

**My recommendation:** Keep view center. It gives the most intuitive default behavior for the common use case (image/map viewer). But I'm happy to switch to (0,0) if you prefer — it simplifies the Matrix property and removes the SetViewSize requirement entirely.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Could we make it so that the operations that need the screen center pass it in? like SetScale? The goal is to make things work without needing it. I like the zoom center, but if we need the origin, then maybe we use that. And things like the wheel zoom, we can zoom around the pointer location. Is that something that could work? I want this to maybe zoom around the cursor location like you do on a maps/image control. Is that something that would simplify, make things not need a view size and still be the best dev/user experience?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Yes, this can absolutely work and would be a nice simplification. Here's the design:

**Core idea:** Switch from view-center origin to (0,0) origin. Every operation that currently uses view center would instead take a `pivot` parameter directly.

### What changes

**Matrix property** — simplifies dramatically:
```csharp
// Before (view-center origin):
Matrix = T(+center) · S · R · T(offset) · T(-center)

// After (0,0 origin):
Matrix = S · R · T(offset)
```
Three operations instead of five. No `_viewWidth`/`_viewHeight` needed.

**AdjustOffsetForPivot** — still needed, but no longer uses view center. The `screenPivot` parameter IS the pivot (no more `d = screenPivot - center`):
```csharp
// The pivot is passed directly from the gesture focal point or cursor location
private void AdjustOffsetForPivot(SKPoint pivot, float oldScale, float newScale, ...)
{
    // pivot is used directly, no center subtraction
}
```

**Where pivots come from** (all already available):
| Operation | Pivot source | Already have it? |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| Pinch zoom | `e.FocalPoint` (finger midpoint) | ✅ Yes |
| Pinch rotate | `e.FocalPoint` | ✅ Yes |
| Scroll/wheel zoom | `e.Location` (cursor position) | ✅ Yes |
| Double-tap zoom | `e.Location` (tap position) | ✅ Yes |
| Fling animation | No pivot needed (just offset) | ✅ N/A |
| Pan | No pivot needed (just offset) | ✅ N/A |

**SetScale/SetRotation** — would take an optional pivot parameter:
```csharp
public void SetScale(float scale, SKPoint? pivot = null)
public void SetRotation(float degrees, SKPoint? pivot = null)
```
If `pivot` is null, scale/rotate around (0,0). If provided, adjust offset to keep that point stationary.

### What gets removed
- `SetViewSize()` method — gone entirely
- `_viewWidth` / `_viewHeight` fields — gone
- The "you must call SetViewSize before reading Matrix" footgun — gone

### Behavior change
The only difference apps would see: at `Scale=2, Offset=(0,0), Rotation=0`, content zooms from top-left instead of center. But in practice, pinch/wheel zoom always provides a focal point, so the user experience is identical — content stays under the finger/cursor.

For `SetScale(2)` without a pivot, it zooms from (0,0). If apps want center zoom, they pass `SetScale(2, new SKPoint(width/2, height/2))`. This is more explicit and doesn't require the tracker to "know" the view size.

### Summary
This is a clean simplification:
- Removes SetViewSize requirement
- Simpler Matrix computation
- More explicit API (pivot is always visible at call site)
- Same user experience for gesture-driven transforms (finger/cursor pivot)
- Better for non-fullscreen use cases (e.g., zooming a sub-region)

Want me to implement this?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: This is great, please do! Update all docs as well. Add tests to cover these new and changed cases.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Done — pushed 97e091de.

**Changes:**
- Removed `SetViewSize()` method and `_viewWidth`/`_viewHeight` fields entirely
- Simplified `Matrix` property to `Scale · Rotate · Translate(offset)` (3 ops, was 5)
- Simplified `AdjustOffsetForPivot` — pivot is used directly, no view center subtraction
- Added optional `pivot` parameter to `SetScale(float scale, SKPoint? pivot = null)` and `SetRotation(float degrees, SKPoint? pivot = null)`
- Gesture-driven transforms (pinch, wheel, double-tap) continue to automatically pivot around the gesture focal point
- Removed `SetViewSize` calls from both Blazor and MAUI samples
- Updated gesture docs — removed all SetViewSize references
- Added 4 new tests: SetScale with/without pivot, SetRotation with pivot, Matrix without SetViewSize
- All 262 tests pass

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Can you now do another 3 agent review like before. Also, make use things are tested and both api and conceptual docs are updated. Make sure the PR title and description match the final changes and not the  past commits. Improve the PR description

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Looking into this — you're asking for:
1. A 3-agent review (architecture, API surface, test coverage)
2. Verify tests are comprehensive
3. Update both API docs (XML) and conceptual docs
4. Update PR title and description to reflect final state

## 3-Agent Review ✅

**Architecture Review** found:
- 🐛 `GestureStarted` event fired multiple times on multi-touch (should only fire on first finger down)
- ✅ Fixed: guards on `_touches.Count == 1`

**API Surface Review** found:
- 🐛 `MinScale`/`MaxScale` cross-validation was one-sided (`MinScale` could be set > `MaxScale`)
- ✅ Fixed: added `value > _maxScale` check to MinScale setter
- 🐛 `PinchState.FromLocations` calculated radius from only `locations[0]` instead of average (incorrect for 3+ fingers)
- ✅ Fixed: now averages distance across all touch points

**Test Coverage Review** found gaps. All addressed:
- ✅ Options validation: 13 `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` checks now tested
- ✅ EventArgs properties: PreviousLocation, ScaleDelta, Speed verified with exact values
- ✅ Transform math: pinch scale assertions now use exact expected values (`≈2.0`, `≈0.5`)
- ✅ Added 28 new tests (290 total, all passing)

## Additional Work ✅

- **Conceptual docs** updated: pivot params on SetScale/SetRotation, GestureStarted clarification
- **API docs** already enabled from prior commit (GenerateDocumentationFile=true)
- **PR title and description** updated to reflect final state of the codebase

## Commits

| SHA | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `36b07ce3` | Fix GestureStarted multi-fire, MinScale validation, pinch radius |
| `ddda2817` | Add 28 new tests + docs updates |

All pushed. 290 tests passing.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Please do a review of the code diff and update the PR description and title. Make sure to look for the staging comment and then also add it as links in the description.

Also remove the regions from the tests as 1 region per test is not useful.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Done — all three items addressed:

**1. PR description & title updated**
- Title: "Add platform-agnostic gesture recognition system (SKGestureDetector + SKGestureTracker)"
- Description rewritten with full architecture, gesture table, design decisions, test coverage, and file summary

**2. Staging links added to description**
- 📖 [Staging Documentation](https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/)
- 🎮 [Staging Blazor Sample](https://mono.github.io/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/326/sample/)

**3. Removed all regions from test files** (commit `11c27885`)
- Removed 118 `#region`/`#endregion` lines across 11 test files
- 204 gesture tests still passing

**Review findings** (from fresh diff review):
- 🔴 `_zoomPrevCumulative` field is dead code (assigned but never read)
- 🟡 Pinch/Rotate events fire even when disabled (only transform is suppressed)
- 🟡 `GetActiveTouchPoints` uses LINQ allocations in 60Hz hot path
- 🟡 `DateTime.Now` default TimeProvider is non-monotonic
- 🟢 Doc refers to `ZoomTo` `targetScale` param but it's actually a multiplier

Let me know which of these to address.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Please fix them all. Also note, the lottie icon in the blazor sidebar is not showing.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
All fixed in `0f0298c0`:

**Review findings fixed:**
1. 🔴 **Dead `_zoomPrevCumulative` field** — removed (was assigned but never read)
2. 🟡 **Event gating** — `PinchDetected`, `RotateDetected`, `FlingDetected` now suppressed when their feature toggles are disabled (previously only the transform was suppressed)
3. 🟡 **LINQ allocations in hot path** — replaced `GetActiveTouchPoints()` LINQ chain with zero-allocation loop + insertion sort (called at 60Hz on every touch move)
4. 🟡 **Non-monotonic TimeProvider** — switched default from `DateTime.Now.Ticks` to `Environment.TickCount64 * TicksPerMillisecond` (immune to DST/NTP adjustments)
5. 🟢 **Doc param names** — fixed `ZoomTo` docs: `factor`/`focalPoint` (not `targetScale`/`pivot`)

**Blazor sidebar fix:**
6. 🆕 **Gestures icon** — added missing `.bi-hand-index-nav-menu` CSS class with hand icon SVG

**Test fix:**
- Fixed flaky `Fling_EventuallyCompletes` test — was mixing real and fake time providers, causing velocity calculation to be wrong under parallel test load

204 gesture tests passing.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Please do a thorough 3 agent review to find any issues. Make sure the API is consistent and follows .NET guidelines. Make sure the docs are up to date.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: Running a thorough 3-agent review now — checking API consistency against .NET guidelines, docs accuracy, and code quality. Will report back with findings.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## 3-Agent Review Complete — All Findings Fixed

### Review Summary

Three parallel review agents analyzed the full gesture system:

| Agent | Focus | 🔴 Critical | 🟡 Moderate | 🟢 Minor |
|-------|-------|-------------|-------------|----------|
| API/.NET Guidelines | Public API surface, naming, XML docs | 2 | 4 | 0 |
| Documentation Accuracy | Markdown docs vs code consistency | 0 | 0 | 3 |
| Code Quality & Bugs | Logic, thread safety, edge cases | 2 | 4 | 5 |

### Critical Fixes (commit `dbe5229d`)

1. **NaN propagation through Clamp** — `Clamp(NaN, min, max)` returned NaN (all NaN comparisons are false), cascading through scale → offset → Matrix → blank canvas. Fixed: NaN now maps to `min`.

2. **Scroll zoom negative scaleDelta** — `1f + delta * 0.1f` with delta ≤ -10 (common on macOS trackpad momentum) produced scaleDelta ≤ 0, causing massive offset teleport. Fixed: clamped to `Math.Max(0.01f, ...)`.

3. **TimeProvider null guard** — Both `SKGestureDetector.TimeProvider` and `SKGestureTracker.TimeProvider` setters now throw `ArgumentNullException` on null.

4. **ZoomTo after Dispose** — Could create a `Timer` after disposal. Now throws `ObjectDisposedException`.

5. **Long press timer leak** — Timer was not stopped when entering Pinching state (2nd finger down). Now explicitly cancelled.

### Minor XML Doc Fixes
- Canvas example: `SetMatrix` → `Save/Concat/Restore` pattern
- `InvalidateVisual()` → `InvalidateSurface()`
- "properties contain" → "property contains" (singular)

### Tests
208 gesture tests passing (was 204, +4 new tests for null guard, dispose, and scroll zoom edge cases).

### Deferred Items (not bugs, acceptable for v1)
- Thread pool timer callbacks (long press, fling, zoom) run without synchronization when `SynchronizationContext` is null — only affects non-UI scenarios (tests, console apps)
- Duplicate `ProcessTouchDown` with same ID silently overwrites — matches platform behavior
- `isMouse` parameter in `ProcessTouchUp` is dead — tracked for future API cleanup
- Zoom animation overwrites concurrent scroll-wheel changes during 250ms window — acceptable UX tradeoff
